purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth subsystem by preventing commands from being queued when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary include directive in a source file.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric added to the store operations.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) to an existing codec binding for a Realtek audio codec.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the remove function of the zorro7xx driver.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the net/mlx5e module.
The commit changes the type of a struct member from unsigned to signed integer.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V, which is a configuration option for the Linux kernel.
The commit introduces a new function to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
The commit message clearly states that it is skipping tests when RTC is not present, indicating a bug-fixing commit.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to a variable called 'bytes' in the echoaudio driver.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks in a CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y kernel.
The commit removes duplicated comments in a header file for KVM's memory management unit (MMU) implementation.

The commit is a revert of a previous change that populated a clock table based on DCFCLK states. The revert is done to prevent an underflow issue caused by how PMFW fills out the table when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC, which has a Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct in the DW PCIe core driver to make it more consistent with other names.
The commit fixes a warning in the clang compiler by changing the format specifier for an unsigned short variable.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label function by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long to do signed compare, avoiding negative offset trigger this warning.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function. This is part of the ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit is a perfective change that improves the code quality by declaring non-global symbols as static.
The commit message clearly states that it drops bogus interrupt flags on MDSS nodes, which suggests a fix for a bug related to incorrect interrupt handling.
The commit message clearly states that the purpose of the change is to prepare for allowing the array size to be changed from a driver.

The commit adds new SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, which is responsible for providing information about the system-on-chip (SoC) and power management integrated circuit (PMIC). The changes are based on the addition of new platforms and boards that use these components.
The commit updates the chip cut recognition in the rtl8xxxu driver to include all possible values from A (0) to P (15).
The commit drops a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks in the UX500 platform.

The commit is a refactoring of the mm/init code to remove #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage. The patch moves the pt_ops macro to adjacent #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL and groups first type usage cases into one.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support I2C nodes.
The commit message clearly explains the purpose of the change, which is to improve the readability of the code by replacing a confusing comment with a more descriptive one.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that may occur if the HW semaphore is not acquired for resource release.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern terminology for clocking.
The commit removes a hack that was used to define IS_SWAPFILE. The change is straightforward and easy to understand.
The commit adds a new feature to the GTP device driver by implementing an echo response function.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It also creates a new function called cpumask_first_zero().
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which would cause a failure message to be displayed on the console.
The commit adds support for a new platform without an amplifier by connecting the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0.
The commit fixes a bug in the zerocopy sendmsg function by failing when unsupported by socket.
The commit message clearly states that it is testing the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, which could prevent errors caused by missing dependencies.
The commit modifies the scripts/checkpatch.pl script to allow macros with complex values to be enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers to improve code architecture and enable easier code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary debug print statements from the codebase.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank, which is a feature that allows the display to enter a low-power state when not in use.
The commit fixes a bug in the kernel's Control Flow Integrity (CFI) feature by updating the function pointer prototype to match reality.
The commit fixes a bug in the pinctrl driver by correcting the name of a property from 'reset' to 'resets'. The change also adds missing 'additionalProperties: false'.
The commit fixes a warning in the GCC compiler by changing an array subscript from 32 to 10.
The commit message clearly states that the change is to simplify code by using a more appropriate function.
The commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring and moves it to a new file called filetable. It also changes the helpers to take a ctx rather than an io_kiocb.
The commit updates the bindings for the media driver to support separate nodes for the G1 and G2 decoders with vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain support.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the codebase.
